blocks.text: DTC P0107: Intake air pressure sensor voltage low

Intake air pressure sensor detects intake air pressure (negative pressure) and converts it to a voltage signal. The intake pressure sensor outputs a lower voltage when the intake pressure (negative pressure) is high (when the throttle valve is fully closed) and a higher voltage when the intake pressure (negative pressure) is low (when the throttle valve is fully open). If the intake pressure sensor voltage remains below a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

blocks.text: Intake air pressure sensor output voltage [intake air pressure sensor] remains below 0.23V for more than 2 seconds.

items.text: Code between PGM-FI ECU and intake pressure sensor (PB TC line)* 1 (PB NA line)* 2 earth fault
Disconnection of cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and intake pressure sensor

blocks.text: *1: Models equipped with a turbo charger
*2: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
